patch.value: pm package init --declarative: scaffold a composeExtension blueprint starter + author-time preflight test

Add a `--declarative` flag to `pm package init` that scaffolds the SDK's flagship declarative authoring loop. Today every scaffolded starter (all 9 --capability variants) authors via imperative `defineExtension({ activate })` and tests via the runtime helpers (activateExtensionForTest/assertRegistered*/runRegistered*/deactivateExtensionForTest). NONE demonstrates `composeExtension`/`defineExtensionBlueprint` (the declarative loop SDK.md calls 'the capstone of the author -> register -> test loop') or the author-time preflight capstone (`assertExtensionPreflight`/`preflightExtension`) or the ergonomic runtime capstone (`createExtensionTestHarness`). A new author who runs `pm package init` never discovers the easiest, best-practice authoring path. The `--declarative` starter generates index.ts as `defineExtensionBlueprint` + `composeExtension(blueprint)` and index.test.ts as `assertExtensionPreflight(blueprint, { identity, target })` (author-time) + `createExtensionTestHarness(module)` (runtime) + teardown. Scoped to package-mode (composeExtension is a runtime SDK value import; bundled extension-only installs stay import-free per SDK.md) and the commands capability (the canonical composeExtension example); other capabilities and extension-mode error clearly. Discovered by dogfooding the full scaffold matrix in a temp workspace (all 9 imperative starters typecheck + pass their generated tests). Goal: project management = context management — make creating new packages as easy and customizable as possible.

Surfaces the declarative composeExtension authoring loop + author-time preflight capstone in the scaffold so package authors discover and adopt the easiest, best-practice path on first `pm package init`.

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: A declarative scaffolded package's index.ts has a runtime VALUE import of composeExtension from @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk (unlike the imperative starter, whose only SDK import is the erased `ExtensionApi` type). So an end-to-end spawnSync dispatch test must link the host SDK into the temp workspace (node_modules/@unbrained/pm-cli -> repo root) or the composed module fails to load — this is exactly why --declarative is package-mode-only (the SDK is a linked dependency) and why the import-free extension-only starters must NOT use composeExtension. The imperative spawn test needs no such link because its type-only import is stripped on load.

patch.value:
## Implementation (2026-06-24)

`pm package init <path> --declarative` scaffolds the SDK's declarative authoring loop.

**Generated files (declarative, package-mode + commands):**
- `index.ts`: `defineCommand` → `defineExtensionBlueprint({ commands, deactivate })` → `export default composeExtension(blueprint)` (exports `pingCommand`/`blueprint`/default module).
- `index.test.ts`: author-time `assertExtensionPreflight(blueprint, { identity, target })` (asserts derived + synthesized `capabilities: ["commands"]`) + runtime `createExtensionTestHarness(extension)` (`assertCommandContract`/`runCommand`/`deactivate`).
- `README.md`: "## Declarative Authoring" section; package.json/manifest.json/tsconfig.json/.gitignore byte-identical to the imperative commands starter.

**Constraints (enforced in `scaffoldExtensionProject`, USAGE exit):**
- package-mode only — `composeExtension` is a runtime SDK value import (extension-only installs stay import-free per SDK.md).
- commands capability only (canonical example); follow-up [[pm-lfdv]] generalizes to all 9 capabilities.
- `--declarative` only valid with `--init/--scaffold`.

**Wiring:** `register-setup.ts` (commander option on both lifecycle + init sub-command, options reader), `extension.ts` (option type, validation, passthrough, `style` in result), `completion.ts` (bash/zsh/fish), `cli-contracts.ts` (`EXTENSION_FLAG_CONTRACTS` + `EXTENSION_INIT_FLAG_CONTRACTS`) + regenerated `tests/fixtures/contracts/full.json`.

**Verification:** 5049 tests, 100/100/100/100 coverage; typecheck (src+packages+examples), static-quality (100% docstrings), docs-skills gates green. Manual dogfood in a temp workspace: scaffold → `tsc --noEmit` clean → `node --test` 2/2 pass → `pm install --project` → `pm <cmd> ping` dispatches the composed module (SDK linked). All three error paths return exit 2 with clear messages.

Shipped `pm package init --declarative`: scaffolds a composeExtension blueprint starter (defineExtensionBlueprint → composeExtension) with an author-time assertExtensionPreflight + createExtensionTestHarness test. Package-mode + commands only (composeExtension is a runtime SDK value import), with clear USAGE errors for extension-mode, non-commands capabilities, and non-init use. Wired through register-setup/extension/completion/cli-contracts (+regenerated contracts snapshot) and documented in SDK.md. 5049 tests, 100/100/100/100 coverage, all quality gates green; manually dogfooded end-to-end (scaffold → typecheck → node --test → install → dispatch). Full-capability-matrix generalization tracked in pm-lfdv.

Added `pm package init --declarative` — a package-mode, commands-capability blueprint starter that scaffolds `defineExtensionBlueprint` → `composeExtension(blueprint)` (index.ts) plus an index.test.ts exercising the author-time `assertExtensionPreflight` capstone and the runtime `createExtensionTestHarness`. Wired the flag through register-setup/extension/completion/cli-contracts; documented in SDK.md.

`pm package init <path> --declarative` generates a typecheck-clean, test-passing composeExtension starter; extension-mode, non-commands `--capability`, and non-init use each return a clear USAGE error; 100% coverage and all quality gates stay green.

Delivered as specified: scaffold generates the declarative starter (style=declarative), all three error paths return exit 2, 5049 tests pass at 100/100/100/100 coverage, typecheck/static-quality/docs-skills/contracts gates green, and the composed module installs + dispatches end-to-end in a dogfooded temp workspace.
